Tomb Raider (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $58,250,803Details
International Box Office $216,700,000
Worldwide Box Office $274,950,803
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,564,032
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $9,124,184
Total $13,688,216
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Lara Croft is the fiercely independent daughter of an eccentric adventurer who vanished when she was scarcely a teen. Now a young woman of 21 without any real focus or purpose, Lara navigates the chaotic streets of trendy East London as a bike courier, barely making the rent, and takes college courses, rarely making it to class. Determined to forge her own path, she refuses to take the reins of her father’s global empire just as staunchly as she rejects the idea that he’s truly gone. Advised to face the facts and move forward after seven years without him, even Lara can’t understand what drives her to finally solve the puzzle of his mysterious death.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$23,633,317 (40.6% of total gross)
Legs:2.46 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:21.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$90,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,854 opening theaters/3,854 max. theaters, 3.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $76,474,166

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 16th, 2018 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
March 16th, 2018 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: March 6th, 2018 (Wide), released as TOMB RAIDER (South Korea)
March 9th, 2018 (Wide) (Indonesia)
March 9th, 2018 (Wide) (Taiwan)
March 9th, 2018 (Wide) (Malaysia)
March 9th, 2018 (Wide) (Hong Kong)
Video Release: June 12th, 2018 by Warner Home Video
July 16th, 2018 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of violence and action, and for some language.
(Rating bulletin 2512 (Cert #51443), 1/31/2018)
Running Time: 118 minutes
Franchise: Lara Croft
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Archaeology, Genocide, Inheritance, Marooned, Missing Person, Non-Chronological, Presumed Dead, Treasure Hunters
Social setting: Secret Societies
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Based on Game
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Pictures, Warner Bros., GK Films, Square Enix Business
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Lara Croft is the fiercely independent daughter of an eccentric adventurer who vanished when she was scarcely a teen. Now a young woman of 21 without any real focus or purpose, Lara navigates the chaotic streets of trendy East London as a bike courier, barely making the rent, and takes college courses, rarely making it to class. Determined to forge her own path, she refuses to take the reins of her father’s global empire just as staunchly as she rejects the idea that he’s truly gone. Advised to face the facts and move forward after seven years without him, even Lara can’t understand what drives her to finally solve the puzzle of his mysterious death.
